feedback.text: Hello, I am very glad to see this very important initiative getting started, I believe all of these are pressing themes in our society. I am also happy to see that the world of videogames is getting treated by being included in some points, such as "Addictive design". As many other citizens suggest, I find the issue of videogame publishers ending support and revoking licenses, practically destroying bought copies to millions of people, to be aligned with the goals of this initiative. The problem resides in unfair, unregulated and often overly broad clauses in EULAs. Taking advantage of these weaknesses is, of course, done to favor an aggressive marketization of the newest videogame these companies are trying to sell. We as a community believe this should be addressed, as it is a violation of consumer rights. We want to own the products we buy without worrying about unfair terms. Hundreds of games have been already destroyed, and many more will be if this keeps being allowed. "Stop Destroying Videogames", another European initiative, is already bringing this point forward, but if DFA decides to support it too the issue could have far greater chances of being solved. Thanks for giving birth to this initiative, with all these pressing issues.
